ddls:
CREATE TABLE "firms" (
  "firm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each firm */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the firm */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the firm */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the firm */,
  "fax_number" TEXT /* Fax number of the firm */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website of the firm */,
  "year_formed" INTEGER /* Year the firm was formed */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the firm */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the firm */,
  "certifications" TEXT /* Certifications held by the firm */,
  "licenses" TEXT /* Licenses held by the firm */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("firm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "executives" (
  "executive_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each executive */,
  "firm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the firm the executive belongs to */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the executive */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the executive */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the executive */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the executive */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Biography of the executive */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("executive_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_executives_firm_id FOREIGN KEY ("firm_id") REFERENCES firms ("firm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service */,
  "service_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_services_service_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_category_id") REFERENCES service_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "firm_services" (
  "firm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the firm */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service */,
  "service_level" TEXT /* Level of service provided (e.g., basic, advanced) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("firm_id", "service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, manager, staff) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "firm_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "firm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the firm */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date of the version */,
  "changes" TEXT /* Changes made in this version */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the changes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_firm_versions_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_firm_versions_firm_id FOREIGN KEY ("firm_id") REFERENCES firms ("firm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_subcategories" (
  "subcategory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subcategory */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category the subcategory belongs to */,
  "subcategory_name" TEXT /* Name of the subcategory */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the subcategory */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subcategory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_subcategories_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES service_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "firm_service_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request */,
  "firm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the firm */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date the request was made */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the request (e.g., pending, approved, rejected) */,
  "requestor" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_firm_service_requests_requestor FOREIGN KEY ("requestor") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_firm_service_requests_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_firm_service_requests_firm_id FOREIGN KEY ("firm_id") REFERENCES firms ("firm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "firm_locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "firm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the firm */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the location */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the location */,
  "zip" TEXT /* Zip code of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_firm_locations_firm_id FOREIGN KEY ("firm_id") REFERENCES firms ("firm_id")
)
column_names: ID of the firm the executive belongs to
Level of service provided (e.g., basic, advanced)
Role of the user (e.g., administrator, manager, staff)
ID of the user who made the changes
ID of the category the subcategory belongs to
Status of the request (e.g., pending, approved, rejected)
ID of the user who made the request
